refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall provide analysis of the worksite terrain.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR combined multiple obligations: analyzing terrain, obstacles, and work-zone representation. To ensure atomicity and solution neutrality, these are separated into distinct sub-FRs focusing solely on what the system must do without implying how.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify that the system can generate and present an analysis of the terrain independently of obstacle and work-zone data, through test cases or simulation data.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall provide analysis of the worksite terrain.
The NGHE solution shall provide analysis of obstacles within the worksite area.
The NGHE solution shall provide representation of the work-zone within the worksite area.

candidate_dps: Generate a digital terrain model representing key site features
Extract and classify terrain features relevant to planning and safety
Create a geospatial map integrating terrain data with safety and operational policies
Provide an interface to access and update the terrain model for planning and execution modules
selected_dp.selected_dp: Generate a digital terrain model representing key site features

potential_sources: Integration of terrain data with safety and operational policies may introduce coupling if not modularized
coupling_check.notes: Selecting a digital terrain model as the DP keeps the solution focused on producing a reusable, independent representation of the worksite terrain. This supports independence from planning and execution functions, which can consume the model as needed.

assumptions_risks: Assumes availability of accurate terrain data sources
Risk that terrain model may not capture all features required by safety and operational policies
Potential complexity in updating the model dynamically as site conditions change
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly separating the terrain model generation from policy integration to maintain modularity and reduce coupling.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the processing and generation of digital terrain models from site data, including key site feature extraction and representation.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives raw sensor and site data necessary for terrain model generation.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives commands or requests to generate or update digital terrain models.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Sends generated digital terrain models and related data to other system components or external users.

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as terrain model generation is a core processing function of NGHE Solution; minimal diffusion as data inputs and outputs are well defined within this module.
Low containment since vehicle focuses on mobility and control, not data processing; would require significant diffusion to other modules for data inputs and outputs.
Teleoperation is focused on remote control and monitoring, not terrain data processing; poor containment and high diffusion expected.
Safety Services is unrelated to terrain model generation; very poor containment and high diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate and specific boundary to contain the digital terrain model generation DP, as it naturally encompasses data processing and site feature extraction functions with minimal diffusion outside the module.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on timely and accurate telemetry data input which may be affected by network issues.
Interface changes in telemetry or network link could impact module integration.
If terrain model generation requires real-time updates, latency in data flow could affect performance.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP 'Generate a digital terrain model representing key site features' directly satisfies the FR 'The NGHE solution shall provide analysis of the worksite terrain' by specifying a concrete method to analyze terrain through digital modeling of key features, aligning well with the acceptance criteria of generating a worksite model/map available to planning and execution functions.

diffusion_risks: Minimal diffusion risk identified as the DP is contained within the NGHE Solution module with a low diffusion score of 2, indicating high cohesion and well-defined interfaces.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module shows high containment (score 8) for the DP, encompassing processing and generation of digital terrain models with clear input (Telemetry, Tasking) and output (Network Link) interfaces. Other modules have low containment and high diffusion scores, supporting the choice of NGHE Solution as the appropriate IM boundary.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible through the availability of generated worksite models/maps to planning and execution functions, as per acceptance criteria. Interfaces for receiving telemetry and tasking data and sending generated models are well defined, enabling traceability and testing.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The ownership candidate 'NGHE Solution' clearly owns the implementation of the DP, with well-scoped module boundaries and minimal diffusion, ensuring clear allocation of responsibility.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed with strong justification, minimal diffusion risk, high containment, and clear ownership allocation. Verification feasibility is confirmed, and no recommended revisions are identified. Given the high safety criticality, the current level of decomposition sufficiently addresses the functional requirement with a well-defined design parameter and implementation module, ensuring traceability and testability. Therefore, further decomposition into sub-FRs is unnecessary at this stage.

assumptions: The DP fully satisfies the FR as per acceptance criteria.
The NGHE Solution module is the appropriate IM with minimal diffusion and high containment.
Verification methods and interfaces are adequately defined for testing.
open_risks: No open risks identified from gate reviews.
evidence_needed: Verification results confirming the generation and availability of digital terrain models to planning and execution functions.
